courses.name: Web Development Basics (Urdu/Hindi)
courses.description: Are you trying to find the best web development course in Urdu or Hindi? You've come to the right place. In this course, you'll learn the basics of web development as a beginner. You'll learn HTML5 basics, the core concepts of CSS3, and the core concepts of JavaScript with the latest standards of javascript like ES6, ES 2021, ES 2022, etc. This course is in Urdu or Hindi so you should understand it very well if you're familiar with these languages.

resources.label: Visual Studio Code (as the IDE/Code Editor)

posts.title: Course Introduction
posts.description: Are you trying to find the best web development course in Urdu or Hindi? You've come to the right place. In this course, you'll learn the basics of web development as a beginner. You'll learn HTML5 basics, the core concepts of CSS3, and the core concepts of JavaScript with the latest standards of javascript like ES6, ES 2021, ES 2022, etc. This course is in Urdu or Hindi so you should understand it very well if you're familiar with these languages.

posts.title: What is Web Development
posts.description: In this lecture, you're going to learn bout what is web development and what a web developer usually does. Also, you'll learn what your goal is at the end of this web development basics course for beginners.

posts.title: Activity: Set up your PC for Web Development
posts.description: In this activity, you're going to set up your PC for web development as a beginner. You're going to install NodeJS, Git, VSCode, and Chrome. The video shows how you can install them and how to identify your PC configuration for web development set up.

posts.title: How to ask questions or get help?
posts.description:
In this video, I'll let you know how to ask questions and make the most out of this web development basics course for beginners in which you'll learn javascript, HTML and CSS.
Here's the link to the discord community:
https://codewithahsan.dev/discord

posts.title: Section Introduction - HTML5
posts.description: In this video you'll learn about what we'll learn in Section 1 of this course. Which is about HTML5. In this course of web development basics for beginners,  you'll learn JavaScript, HTML and CSS and a lot of cool tools and techniques to use as a Web Developer.

posts.title: Your first Web Application
posts.description: In this video, you'll create your very first web application. You'll learn how to use VSCode to write your first web app and how to run it on Chrome.

posts.title: Structure of a Web Page
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn how web pages are built with HTML, what is the purpose of the `head`, `body and other related HTML tags as well. We'll go through an example webpage in this video

posts.title: Headings
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about Heading Tags and how to use them. You'll also learn why they are important for search engines and how to structure your code with proper heading levels.

posts.title: Activity: Use VSCode Live Preview Extension
posts.description: In this video, you're going to learn how to set up VSCode's Live Preview server so we don't have to switch between applications (chrome and VSCode) for simple things.

posts.title: Tags VS Attributes
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the difference between tags vs attributes in HTML with some examples. You'll also learn a quick way of adding attributes to anchor tags and images as an example.

posts.title: Paragraphs
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about how to work with HTML paragraphs. You'll learn about the `p` tag and the `pre` tag and how they preserve or do not preserve white spaces. You'll also learn the alignment of it

posts.title: Formatting Text
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about how we can format HTML elements. We have some special HTML tags like `strong`, `em`, `pre`, `mark`, `bold` etc.

posts.title: Styles
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the different ways you can use CSS Styles in HTML. You'll learn about inline styles vs the style tag. You'll also see some examples of the styles we can use in HTML and how we can reuse the styles.

posts.title: Colors
posts.description: In this lecture you'll learn about how to use Colors in HTML. You'll learn about the different ways we can define the colors to be used. We'll talk about rgb, rgba, and hex colors. I'll also show you https://www.materialpalette.com/ where you can get inspiration from some cool color combinations

posts.title: Lists
posts.description: In this lecture, you'll learn about how to use Lists in HTML. You're going to learn about unordered lists, ordered lists and some cool attributes to work with these lists.

posts.title: Images
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about HTML images and how to use the img tag to work with images. You'll also learn how to style the images and how to use image links.

posts.title: Forms Introduction
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about how to work with HTML Forms. HTML Forms are very powerful and are used in almost every web application you'll use today. You'll learn about the form, some inputs and how to submit the form.

posts.title: Activity: Using Starter Files
posts.description: In this activity, you'll learn to use the starter files for the next lectures. This is important as for some lectures, you'll need to have a set of files available to work with.

posts.title: Form Elements
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about how to work with different Form Elements inside the form. We'll talk about certain types of inputs, how you register and name those inputs and a bit about what attributes can be used within those form elements.

posts.title: Input Types
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about how to work with different form inputs. We'll talk about the most common type of input elements used including but not limited to Text, Password, Date, Month, Week, Color, and File.

posts.title: Links
posts.description: In this video, you will learn how to work with links in an HTML file. You'll learn about what the href attribute does and how to open the link in a new tab. You'll also learn about the difference between absolute vs relative links

posts.title: Entities
posts.description: In this video, you will learn how to work with HTML entities which are especial characters to use in an HTML file. You'll learn about the less than, greater than, copyright and other special entities.

posts.title: Tables
posts.description: In this video, you will learn how to work with tables in an HTML file. You'll learn about table rows, table data, table headings and the related elements to create a table in HTML.

posts.title: Block VS Inline Elements
posts.description: In this video, you will learn the differences between Block vs Inline elements in HTML. You'll learn about the different types of block elements and different types of inline elements. You'll also see how do we structure our HTML layout using these two.

posts.title: Audio & Video Tags
posts.description: In this video, you will learn how to work with the audio and video tags in an HTML file. You'll learn about how to show controls on an audio vs a video file. And how to autoplay an audio or a video file. You'll also learn how to mute them on page load.

posts.title: Semantic HTML
posts.description: In this video, you will learn the what is Semantic HTML and why can't we just use div and span elements everywhere. You'll learn why it is important to know Semantic HTML tags and which tags to use when.

posts.title: Assignment - Business Card
posts.description:
Create a new project and create the following business card using HTML and CSS.  
You're supposed to have the following things as per mock:
posts.article:
Create a new project and create the following business card using HTML and CSS.  
You're supposed to have the following things as per mock:
- Heading saying "Business Card"
- Profile Image
- Name & Email
- Skills (put any random skills)
- Interests (put any random interests)
- Social links at the bottom with images
  - Clicking on the image should navigate to the corresponding link in a new tab

![Assignment](https://github.com/AhsanAyaz/slides/raw/main/talks/assets/images/web-dev-basics/assignments/basic-business-card.png)

posts.title: Section Introduction - CSS3
posts.description: In this video, you will learn what we are going to learn in this section. We'll talk about CSS3, Flexbox, Grid, mobile responsiveness and some other interesting topics.

posts.title: Different ways of using CSS
posts.description: In this video, you will learn how to use CSS using 3 different ways. You'll learn about the inline-styles, the style tag in HTML and using external style sheets.

posts.title: Classes vs ID
posts.description: In this video, you will learn how to use the class and ID selectors in css. You'll learn why and when to use ID selectors in CSS and when to use the ID selectors. We also discuss when do we really put an ID or a class in HTML in the context of aiming to style the elements.

posts.title: Combinator Selectors & Grouping Selectors
posts.description: In this video, you will learn how to use universal selectors, combinator selectors, descendent selectors and a couple of more types of selectors.

posts.title: Pseudo Classes
posts.description: In this video, you will learn what are pseudo classes in CSS and how to target different pseudo states. We will discuss the most common pseudo-classes used in HTML and CSS for real-world use cases

posts.title: Pseudo Elements
posts.description: In this video, you will learn what are pseudo elements in CSS and how to target different pseudo-elements using CSS selectors. We will talk about ::first-letter, ::first-line, ::before, ::after, and ::marked elements etc.

posts.title: Colors
posts.description: In this video, we're going to discuss CSS colors and some color properties. We'll learn about text color, background color, border color, etc with some real-world examples.

posts.title: Borders
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about CSS borders and how to use them. You'll learn about both the short-hand syntax for CSS borders and how to use different border properties.

posts.title: Margins
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about CSS margins and how to use them. You'll learn both the short-hand syntax for CSS margins and how to use different margin properties individually. You'll also learn how to use the chrome debugger to inspect margins of an element.

posts.title: Paddings
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about CSS paddings and how to use them. You'll learn both the short-hand syntax for CSS paddings and how to use different margin properties individually. You'll also learn how to use the chrome debugger to inspect the padding of an element.

posts.title: CSS Box Model
posts.description: In this video, we'll discuss the CSS Box Model. We'll discuss what are the 4 different factors of the Box Model, how to understand the concepts around it, and how box-sizing: border-box; is a game changer to make things easier for developers.

posts.title: Opacity
posts.description: In this video, we'll discuss what is opacity. How to make an HTML element transparent using opacity. And we'll also discuss how you can style different elements for certain UX practices using opacity.

posts.title: Styling Text
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn how to style the text in HTML using different techniques including alignment, shadows, and some other neat CSS tricks.

posts.title: Display & Visibility
posts.description: In this video, you're going to learn what are the differences between CSS display and visibility features. You'll see some real-world examples to understand the differences between both of them.

posts.title: Fonts
posts.description: In this video, we're going to discuss about CSS Fonts. We're going to talk about font-family, font-size, font-variant, font-weight, font-style and font-stretch

posts.title: Icons
posts.description: In this video, we're going to discuss CSS Icons. We'll look into how to use Material Icons and FontAwesome icons in your web applications.

posts.title: Activity: Building a Horizontal & a Vertical Nav Bar
posts.description: In this video, we're going to build two different navigation bars. We'll build one horizontal Navigation Bar (Navbar) using HTML and CSS and we'll do the same for the vertical navbar. You'll also see some basic usage of flex box to understand how we build the layout. Once you've followed it, try building and submitting your own version of navbars. Style it a bit differently.

posts.title: Positions
posts.description: In this video, we're going to discuss CSS Positions. This topic is something even the senior web developers struggle with some times. We're going to talk about position relative, position fixed and position absolute in particular.

posts.title: Box Shadows
posts.description: In this video, we're going to discuss CSS Positions. This topic is something even the senior web developers struggle with some times. We're going to talk about position relative, position fixed and position absolute in particular.

posts.title: Width & Height
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn what happens when we give an element width and a height. We'll see what to be cautious about when doing that. And what are some good use cases to use width and height in px vs in % etc.

posts.title: Z-Index
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the z-index style in CSS. You'll learn why we use Z Index and what to be cautious about. You'll also learn the default behavior of CSS when there's no z-index provided and how to overlap items using z-index.

posts.title: Overflow
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about CSS overflow. You'll learn how to use it with max width or max height to create a scrolling effect. You'll also learn about overflow hidden vs overflow scroll vs overflow auto with some real examples.

posts.title: Forms and CSS
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn to build an HTML form with CSS. You'll learn how to style inputs, select elements and buttons, and how to construct a good-looking HTML form.

posts.title: !important styles & CSS Specificity
posts.description: In this video, we'll look at what is CSS Specificity and which style has preference over the others in certain cases. We also discussed the !important keyword in CSS and how it overrides the specificity of the CSS styles when used.

posts.title: Activity: Building a portfolio website
posts.description: In this video, we're doing an activity in which we're creating a basic portfolio site using HTML and CSS. We'll not be using flexbox in this video but would rather use some other techniques like margin and text alignment to align things.
posts.article:
### Assignment

Create a new project and create the following portfolio site using HTML and CSS (without flexbox)
You're supposed to have the following things as per mock:

#### Mockup

![Assignment](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/AhsanAyaz/slides/main/talks/assets/images/web-dev-basics/assignments/portfolio-without-css.png)

posts.title: Section Introduction - CSS Advanced
posts.description: In this video, you'll get to know what we're going to learn in the third section of this course. We've already discussed HTML, and CSS3 basics. This section will be about some advanced concepts in CSS.

posts.title: Flexbox: Flex Container
posts.description: In this video, you're going to learn Flexbox and specifically Flex Container. You'll learn how to use the flex styles on the container element to position, wrap, and align the contents using Flexbox.

posts.title: Flexbox: Flex Items
posts.description: In this video, you're going to learn Flexbox and specifically Flex Items. You'll learn how to use the flex styles on the items in a flexbox layout element to position, wrap, and align the items themselves using Flexbox. We're going to cover orders, self-alignment, etc.

posts.title: Activity: Responsive Gallery using Flexbox
posts.description: In this activity, we'll build a responsive CSS image gallery using Flexbox. We'll use a 4-column layout on desktop, 2 column layout on Tablet, and 1 column layout for mobile devices.

posts.title: Grid
posts.description: In this video, you're going to learn what CSS Grids are, and how to work with them. We'll look at mainly the properties you can apply to CSS Grid containers, and how you could build different layouts.

posts.title: CSS Variables
posts.description: In this video, we're going to learn CSS Variables and how to use CSS Variables. We'll look into global CSS Variables, how to override them within media queries, and how they become reusable.

posts.title: Transforms
posts.description: In this video, we're going to learn CSS Transforms and how to use CSS Transforms in a Web Application. We'll look into translateX, translateY, translate, scaleX, scaleY, scale, rotateX, rotateY, rotate, skewX, skewY, skew, and the matrix transform with practical examples.

posts.title: Animations
posts.description: In this video, we're going to learn CSS Animations. We'll learn with some real world examples on how to work with CSS Animations and how to use CSS keyframes to do so. We'll discuss about the CSS Keyframes using from and to properties, and using different percentages as well. We'll discuss different properties to use with CSS Animations like animation-duration, animation-timing-function etc.

posts.title: CSS Media Queries
posts.description: In this video, you're going to briefly learn CSS Media queries and how to build responsive websites with media queries. In the video, we go through a grid layout that we want to work with for tablets and for mobile screens using CSS media queries.

posts.title: Activity: Building a Landing Page from Figma Design
posts.description: In this video, we're going to build a landing page using a Figma Design. You'll learn how to work with Figma to extract images and export them. You'll also learn how to work with Figma to inspect elements to figure out CSS properties to implement the pixel-perfect design. You'll also learn how to find out gaps, margins, paddings, etc using Figma Design.

posts.title: Section Introduction - JavaScript
posts.description: In this video, you'll get to know what we're going to learn in the fourth section of this course. We've already discussed HTML, CSS3 basics, and CSS Advanced. This section will start learning javascript and we'll start with the first few basics concepts of javascript

posts.title: Introduction to JavaScript
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn what javascript is, why it is used, and what the background is in general for javascript. We'll see some basic examples of javascript and how to use it.

posts.title: How to use JavaScript
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn what are the different ways of using javascript in a web application. You're going to learn about the script tag, the console, and using external scripts as well to work in web applications.

posts.title: Statements
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn javascript statements. We will discuss what are statements, keywords, and expressions in javascript. We'll look at different examples of javascript statements and how to use them properly.

posts.title: JavaScript Syntax
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn the javascript syntax. You're going to learn how to write javascript. You'll learn variables, values, and the way we structure our javascript code.

posts.title: JavaScript Variables
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about javascript variables. You'll know the difference between var, let, and const. Learn easily in Urdu language what javascript variables are, what is the concept behind them and how to use them.

posts.title: JavaScript Comments
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript Comments. You'll learn single line comments and multi-line comments and how to use them properly. JavaScript comments are used to comment out code that we don't need to run by the compiler.

posts.title: Javascript Arithmetic Operators
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn how to perform arithmetic operations in JavaScript. You'll learn how to add, subtract, multiply etc. You'll also learn the javascript syntax to use arithmetic operators in JavaScript.

posts.title: JavaScript Assignment Operators
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn the assignment operators in javascript. You'll learn ++, +-, +=, -= and more operators to use in javascript.

posts.title: JavaScript Comparison Operators
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn javascript comparison operators. You'll learn about strict vs non-strict equality check in JavaScript, less than, greater than, less than and equals, greater than and equals and not equals operators.

posts.title: JavaScript Data Types
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript data types. You'll learn boolean, string, number, array, objects, undefined, null and more.

posts.title: JavaScript Functions
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript functions. You'll learn what are arguments or parameters and what are return values. You'll also learn what is the default return value of a function. And you'll learn all this with some real examples.

posts.title: JavaScript Objects
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn javascript objects. You'll learn what objects are, what are properties and methods and how to access a property or method of an object. You'll also learn how to call an object's method. This video about javascript object in hindi is super useful and easy to learn.

posts.title: JavaScript Boolean, Truthy & Falsy values
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about JavaScript boolean, truthy and falsy values. You'll learn about some interesting facts about the Boolean function, Boolean type, and some use cases of the truthy and falsy values

posts.title: JavaScript Strings
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn all about JavaScript strings. You'll learn about the different methods to use in JavaScript strings, you'll also learn some tips and tricks when using those methods around whitespaces.

posts.title: JavaScript Numbers
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about Numbers in JavaScript. You'll learn what kind of number values we can assign to variables in javascript, and how to work with numbers in general. You'll also learn about methods like parseInt, and Number.toFixed() etc.

posts.title: JavaScript Dialog Boxes
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript dialog boxes like prompt, confirm, and alert. We'll see some interesting examples of how to use these dialog boxes to take user input using these dialogs and when to show information from some of these.

posts.title: JavaScript If-Else Conditionals
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn the If-Else conditionals. These conditionals are used to write different business logics based on different conditions. These are probably your first statements to implement program control flow. Learn how to use if, else-if and else conditions to control the program's flow.

posts.title: Activity: Building a basic addition calculator with JavaScript
posts.description: In this video, you'll build a simple addition calculator with JavaScript. You'll take input from the user from dialog boxes and will work with if-else conditionals to build a basic addition calculator.

posts.title: JavaScript Switch-Case Conditionals
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn the switch-case conditionals. I spent more time on this video to make it comprehensive with an example to follow. Most of the video is working on the program and in the end we discuss about the switch-case statements.

posts.title: JavaScript Arrays
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn javascript arrays. This is going to be an overview of the JavaScript Arrays including how we can create them, what are the most common methods and properties etc.

posts.title: JavaScript Array Methods
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the Array methods. In the previous video we discussed about the general Array methods and properties. In this one, we're going to learn about almost all the important Array methods you will usually use in a JavaScript project.

posts.title: JavaScript Arrays & Sorting
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript Arrays and how you sort the arrays. You will also learn how to reverse sort the arrays and how to sort arrays having objects.

posts.title: JavaScript For Loop
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the first kind of loop in JavaScript. That is the For Loop. This is the easiest loop to learn in JavaScript and is used in other programming languages with slight variations as well. We're going to learn it with some real-world examples.

posts.title: JavaScript While Loop
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the While Loop. This is yet another interesting loop that exists in JavaScript and is used in other programming languages. We're going to learn it with some real-world examples and will compare it against the classic For Loop.

posts.title: JavaScript Do While Loop
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the Do-While loop which is quite similar to the while loop in javascript and other languages. You will also learn some real-world use cases about when to use these.

posts.title: JavaScript For In Loop
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn the For-In Loop in JavaScript which is majorly used to traverse the objects (key-value pairs). We'll go through some real world examples and some gotchas that you need to be aware about.

posts.title: JavaScript For Of Loop
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn the For Of loop in JavaScript. This loop is used mostly on Arrays and iterables when we're 100% sure about running the loop on the entire array instead of running a classic for loop.

posts.title: JavaScript Regexes - Part 1
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about JavaScript regexes. Regex is a powerful tool that allows you to search text from a string, validate if a string contains some specific word or has a specific pattern. And it is used widely in programming languages and to build tools and applications. We will learn through different real-world examples.

posts.title: JavaScript Regexes - Part 2
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn more about JavaScript regexes. We'll go through collections in regex and some example regexes we can work with.

posts.title: JavaScript Break and Continue
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn how to use the `break` and `continue` statements in JavaScript. We'll go through some real-world examples where these are going to be useful.

posts.title: Activity: Pugam Game in JavaScript
posts.description: In this video, we'll create the classic "pugam ki pugaayi" game in JavaScript. This is a famous thing in Pakistan (and probably India too) that is used to decide the loser among a group of people. In this game, we'll have a human player with two computer players and we'll see who wins :) This is a fun project to build and work on.

posts.title: JavaScript Hoisting
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript Hoisting. It is a concept asked by many interviewers in the interviews. You'll learn what is a scope in JavaScript, and how does hoisting work with the `var` keyword against the `let` or `const` keywords.

posts.title: Javascript this keyword
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the `this` keyword in JavaScript. It is one of the most common topics that is asked about in the interviews. And it takes a bit of time to  get used to the concept as well.

posts.title: JavaScript Dates
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the Date object in javascript. You'll learn how to create dates, how to get minutes, hours, etc from the Date object with some real-world examples.

posts.title: JavaScript DOM
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about the famous DOM that everybody keeps talking about in when it comes to Web Technologies. We're going to look at some examples for DOM Manipulation and how to access it for specific use cases.

posts.title: JavaScript Classes
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript classes. You'll learn how to use classes to create objects. You'll also learn about object comparison with the equality operators. And you'll learn about the shared methods in JavaScript classes

posts.title: What is JSON
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about what is JSON. You'll learn about the JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) and some examples to understand the concept better. As JSON is one of the most famous formats of storing data today, it is almost essential to learn about this.

posts.title: JavaScript Debugging
posts.description: In this video, you'll how to debug JavaScript code in the browser. We'll be using Chrome for debugging but you can use other browsers for similar use cases as well. We'll go through the different debugging options like Step Into, Step Over, Step Out, Break Points, Log Points, Conditional Breakpoints and some other concepts as well.

posts.title: Section Introduction
posts.description: In this video, you'll get to know what we're going to learn in the fifth section of this course. We've already covered the basics of javascript in the previous section. In this one, we dive a bit deeper into more concepts of JavaScript.

posts.title: JavaScript Callbacks
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn about JavaScript callbacks. This is your first step in asynchronous programming.We'll discuss what are javascript callbacks, how to use javascript callbacks and javascript callbacks in Urdu/Hindi.

posts.title: JavaScript Promises
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript Promises with some real-world example. We'll discuss how to use Promises, what are the states available in JavaScript Promises and how to handle the pending, fulfilled and rejected states. We'll also discuss how to catch errors when working with Promises.

posts.title: JavaScript Async Await
posts.description: In this video, you'll learn JavaScript Promises with SUPER POWERS. I.e. you'll learn about Async Await. This is an amazing way of working with promises but you write the code in a "synchronous" way instead of an "asynchronous way". Let me demystify this concept for you.

posts.title: Activity - Random Users List App
posts.description: In this activity, we're basically combining all our knowledge so far to create a random users list application that works with a real API. We use Promises, CSS, and what not. You'll absolutely enjoy this activity.

posts.title: Wrap up
posts.description: We're wrapping up the course in this video :) This was so much fun and engaging and I loved all of your responses, questions, and enthusiasm. I'll be working on the website more to be able to show your assignments and progress. Let me know which course do you want me to work on next.

courses.name: Practical ReactJS Essentials in Urdu/Hindi
courses.description:
Dive into the world of modern web development as you master React's fundamental concepts, including component-based architecture, state management, and hooks, enabling you to confidently build dynamic, responsive user interfaces for cutting-edge applications.
[Enroll in Course](https://codewithahsan.teachable.com/p/practical-react-essentials)
courses.outline: Embark on a comprehensive journey into modern web development with our ReactJS course. You’ll explore essential concepts such as component-based architecture, state management, and Hooks, all while mastering the techniques needed to build high-performing, responsive user interfaces. By the end of this course, you’ll be equipped with the knowledge and confidence to create cutting-edge applications that showcase React’s power and flexibility.

authors.name: Muhammad Ahsan Ayaz
authors.bio: Muhammad Ahsan Ayaz is a Google Developers Expert in Angular and a Software Architect at Scania. He loves helping the startup ecosystem and product owners bring their ideas to life using JavaScript, Angular & Web Technologies. He has built several open-source projects that he maintains, speaks at events, writes articles, and makes video tutorials. https://bio.link/muhd_ahsanayaz

courses.name: Angular in 90ish minutes
courses.outline: Learn the core concepts of Angular in just 90 minutes. We'll cover standalone components, Angular Signals, Components, Directives, Services, Pipes, and more...

authors.name: Muhammad Ahsan Ayaz
authors.bio: Muhammad Ahsan Ayaz is a Google Developers Expert in Angular and a Software Architect at Scania. He loves helping the startup ecosystem and product owners bring their ideas to life using JavaScript, Angular & Web Technologies. He has built several open-source projects that he maintains, speaks at events, writes articles, and makes video tutorials. https://bio.link/muhd_ahsanayaz

posts.title: Making HTTP calls in Angular Services
Passing data from parent to child component

courses.name: Full Stack TypeScript - Let's build a "Threads" app with Angular & Nest JS
courses.outline: In this comprehensive tutorial, we'll dive deep into Full Stack TypeScript, as we build a real-world application, namely a "Threads" app, using Angular and Nest JS. This video is the perfect resource for those eager to learn Full Stack development with a TypeScript twist, so whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting out, there's something here for everyone!

posts.title: Creating users on the fly for creating comments
Rendering top-level comments from the API call
Setting up Tailwind CSS for Angular
Using Mongoose Models in API calls
Validating request bodies calls in NestJS

courses.name: MERN Stack Crash Course for beginners (MongoDB, ExpressJs, React, NodeJS in 8 hours)
courses.description: Build a full-fledged MERN stack application from scratch as you follow along this video containing a series of recorded videos, and live streams. You'll be creating a Notes application using React, MongoDB, Express JS, and NodeJS.

chapters.name: MERN Stack Crash Course Default Chapter

posts.title: Deploying react app to GitHub pages

courses.name: Design Patterns in JavaScript
courses.description: In this series, you'll learn about **JavaScript Design Patterns** with React app examples. This is highly inspired by [patterns.dev](https://patterns.dev)
courses.outline:
### 🤔 Who is this for?
If you're a JavaScript engineer and want to dive more into _Design Patterns_ to strengthen your JavaScript skills, this is the course for you. We'll go through some core Design Patterns within JavaScript that you can use in your routine work. It really doesn't matter what experience with JavaScript you have, this could be a refresher for you even if you're a senior engineer.

### 👉🏻 What will I learn?
You'll be learning some famous Design Patterns with the following features:
- Understanding of the Design Systems such as Singleton Pattern, Proxy Pattern, etc.
- Implementation of the design systems in JavaScript. 
- A real-world examples using the Design Systems.

courses.name: JavaScript Beginners Series
courses.description: In this course, you'll learn the core concepts and APIS in JavaScript. For example, variables, loops, conditionals, arrays, strings and much more. If you're a fresher diving into JavaScript, this is for you.
courses.outline:
### 🤔 Who is this for?
If you're a fresh JavaScript engineer and have no hands-on experience with JavaScript. This course is for you. If you're an experienced JavaScript engineer, you still might find some things useful in this series.

### 👉🏻 What will I learn?
You'll be learning the core JavaScript principles. Such as:
- Variables
- Conditionals
- Loops
- Arrays
- Strings
- Objects
- OOP in JS
- and more...

courses.name: React Redux Toolkit tutorial for beginners
courses.outline:
This is the only React Redux Toolkit tutorial you need to get started with React and TypeScript. In this video, we start with React using Vite, start working on including Redux Toolkit in the project, configure the redux slices, create redux actions, create redux selectors, dispatch redux actions from our components, and also create an async thunk using the rtk (Redux tool kit). This short yet informative tutorial will save you hours of searching the internet to really get started with these.
Code repository:
https://github.com/code-with-ahsan/react-rtk
